# Stop Detection

> Detect and record periods when a vehicle is stationary, capturing stop location, start time, end time, and duration, to support idle time analysis, delivery dwell time reporting, and route compliance.

**Category:** Workflow · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** gps · tracking · stop · idle · dwell · fleet · report

## What this does

Detect and record periods when a vehicle is stationary, capturing stop location, start time, end time, and duration, to support idle time analysis, delivery dwell time reporting, and route compliance.

Specifies 3 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **device_id** *(hidden, required)* — Device being monitored
- **stop_latitude** *(number, optional)* — Latitude of the stop location
- **stop_longitude** *(number, optional)* — Longitude of the stop location
- **stop_start_time** *(datetime, optional)* — Timestamp when movement ceased and the stop began
- **stop_end_time** *(datetime, optional)* — Timestamp when movement resumed and the stop ended
- **stop_duration_seconds** *(number, optional)* — Total duration of the stop in seconds
- **stop_gap_seconds** *(number, optional)* — Minimum seconds of stillness required before a period is classified as a stop

## What must be true

- **rule_1:** A stop is detected when the device remains below the motion speed threshold for at least stop_gap_seconds
- **rule_2:** Short stationary periods below stop_gap_seconds (e.g., red lights, traffic) are not classified as stops
- **rule_3:** A stop ends and a new trip may begin when the device's speed exceeds the motion threshold again
- **rule_4:** Stop location is the coordinates at which movement first ceased; minor GPS drift during a stop is ignored
- **rule_5:** Stop records include the reverse-geocoded address when available
- **rule_6:** Stops that overlap with geofence zones are annotated with the zone name for context
- **rule_7:** Engine hours and fuel data at stop boundaries are recorded to support idle fuel consumption analysis

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Brief Halt Ignored** — when device speed dropped to zero but resumed movement within stop_gap_seconds, then Short halt ignored; no stop record created (traffic light, junction, etc.).
- **Stop Detected** — when device speed has been below the motion threshold for >= stop_gap_seconds; device was previously in a moving state, then Stop record started; location, time, and sensor values at stop onset captured.
- **Stop Ended** — when device resumes movement after a recorded stop; device speed exceeds motion threshold, then Stop record closed with end time and duration; available for stop reports.

## Errors it can return

- `STOP_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND` — The device referenced does not exist

## Events

**`stop.detected`** — Vehicle has come to a stop for longer than the minimum stop threshold
  Payload: `device_id`, `stop_latitude`, `stop_longitude`, `stop_start_time`, `geofence_ids`

**`stop.ended`** — Vehicle has resumed movement after a recorded stop
  Payload: `device_id`, `stop_start_time`, `stop_end_time`, `stop_duration_seconds`

## Connects to

- **trip-detection** *(required)* — Stops are the inverse of trips; both use the same motion state machine
- **gps-position-ingestion** *(required)* — Position speed values drive stop detection
- **geofence-management** *(recommended)* — Stops inside geofences are annotated with zone context
- **fleet-scheduled-reports** *(recommended)* — Stop records feed into scheduled stop reports
